purpose: Establish a trustworthy verification baseline before any Wave 9+ code change, and produce a clean-environment bootstrap proof against the new requirements-lock.txt.

existing_venv_runs.run_official_checks_py_end_to_end: All official checks passed (ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy src, pytest -q, validate_plan_structure.py, actionlint against all 6 workflow files).

fresh_venv_bootstrap_proof.environment: genuinely fresh venv created via `python -m venv`, dependencies installed ONLY from requirements-lock.txt plus `pip install --no-deps -e .`

fresh_venv_bootstrap_proof.mypy_src: Success: no issues found in 151 source files
fresh_venv_bootstrap_proof.pytest_full_suite: 944 passed, 18 deselected, duration_seconds 297.60 (938 baseline + 6 new tests added this session for the requirements.md column-count check)
fresh_venv_bootstrap_proof.conclusion: The lockfile is complete and sufficient -- a fresh Python environment built only from requirements-lock.txt (no floating version resolution, no reliance on an already-populated global environment) reproduces every official check green.

ops_010_relevance.prior_status: PARTIAL -- two of three known hang variants already fixed/worked-around; one sequence-dependent create_work_clone() subprocess hang in test_supervisor_loop.py's full-file run never reproduced in isolation, root cause not yet isolated.
ops_010_relevance.new_evidence_this_session: 4 consecutive full-suite runs this session (3 in the existing .venv, 1 in a genuinely fresh venv) all completed clean with zero hangs and zero orphaned git/python processes (tasklist-checked after each of the 3 .venv runs).
ops_010_relevance.honest_assessment: This is corroborating evidence that the issue is real but low-frequency/intermittent, consistent with the row's own prior finding that it never reproduces in isolation and is plausibly linked to concurrent host load rather than a deterministic code bug. This does NOT constitute a root-cause fix and does not by itself justify reclassifying OPS-010 to IMPLEMENTED -- the row stays PARTIAL with this new evidence appended, per this project's own discipline against overclaiming from a small, if clean, sample.

artifacts_produced_this_phase: plans/requirements.md (5 malformed table rows fixed: OPS-009, EFF-004, ORC-006, VER-005, SCL-005)
